responses.text: During the pandemic in Japan, the number of suicides among women and children has increased.
The number of suicides in Japan reached 21,077 in 2020, the first increase in 11 years, and even 2.5 times the number of deaths from COVID-19.
Prime Minister of Japan Yoshihide Suga decided to introduce the post of Minister to combat loneliness and isolation.
During the pandemic, people who were 95% close to suicide faced new challenges.
The number of suicides in Japan fell by 14% during the first wave of the pandemic in February-June 2020
About 40% of suicide prevention organizations in Japan have temporarily closed or reduced their working hours due to the epidemic
Since July 2020, the number of suicides in Japan has increased, with the number of female suicides increasing, especially among teenagers.
Prime Minister Yoshihide Suga told Tetsushi Sakamoto to identify relevant problems and propose corresponding prevention and control policies to address the issue of loneliness.
A long-term economic downturn caused by the epidemic could lead to a rebound in the suicide rate
Japan ’ s October suicides are “more than all Coronavirus deaths this year”
Unemployment, social isolation and concern for the future are contributing to the rise in suicide cases in Japan.
Suicide ranked first among causes of death among 20-24 year olds in Japan
The psychological burden associated with the coronavirus pandemic contributed to the rise in suicide rates.
Depression has also increased in the pandemic in Japan.
The family environment in Japan has changed due to the coronavirus epidemic.
The suicide rate in Japan increased by 16% in July-October last year
The economic problems brought about by COVID-19 contributed to the increase in the number of suicides.
The number of suicides in Japan increased dramatically among women and children during the 2nd wave of the pandemic
Japan's labor and social welfare loopholes have been exposed due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Suicide ranked first among causes of death among 25-29 year olds in Japan
The increase in mental trauma is one of the reasons for suicide.
Official Japanese figures show there were 2,153 suicides last month, more than the total number of deaths from coronavirus this year
Palestinian Authority officials, including Mahmoud Abbas, Riyad al-Maliki and Saeb Erekat, have repeatedly announced that they reject the Arab agreement with the Zionists.
Palestinian leaders called the joint statement of the United States, Israel and the United Arab Emirates as the completion of the "Deal of the Century".

Two people were injured when a Palestinian rocket fired in protest against the normalization of relations between the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain and Israel, and Israel responded by bombing the Gaza Strip.
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for an extraordinary meeting of the Arab League as well as an urgent meeting of Palestinian leaders and groups to discuss the consequences of the agreement.
The Palestinian Government of National Accord will not allow Israel to benefit from the internal division of Palestine

Palestinian Authority Prime Minister Mohammed Ashtiyah pointed to the agreement between the United Arab Emirates and Israel to normalize relations and halt the plan for the annexation of the Palestinian territories.
The Palestinian Islamic Jihad Movement issued a statement complaining against the recent hostile actions of the Palestinian Authority of Mahmoud Abbas against Palestinian protesters in the West Bank.
Palestinian President Mahmud Abbas expressed "strong opposition and condemnation" to the peace agreement.
The Palestinian Authority rejected the diplomatic actions of the Persian Gulf states vis-à-vis the Zionist regime
Abbas opposed the "deal of the century" to resolve the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, proposed by US President Donald Trump
Palestinians protest against Bahrain's establishment of diplomatic relations with Israel

Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas said he had informed Egypt that the Palestinians would oppose the deal of the century and that security services would not be ordered to prevent demonstrators from reaching the call lines.

The Palestinian leadership has said it can no longer see the United States as a mediator in peace talks with the Israelis and will be forced to freeze recognition of Israel until it recognizes the State of Palestine.
Hamas calls for the Palestinian people to stand against the annexation of the West Bank
Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas has said he will no longer accept U.S. plans for a Middle East peace process.
Palestinian Authority president rejects Fatah member’s anti-Saudi statements

The Foreign Ministry of the Palestinian Authority also condemned the aggressive attack by the Zionist army on the leaders of the Islamic Jihad in Damascus and Gaza and called on the international community and the United Nations to act to stop the crimes of the regime.
Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas has said it no longer has a commitment to implement previous agreements with Israel and the United States, including those on security.
The Palestinian Authority no longer represents the Palestinian people and their goals

The Palestinian groups strongly condemned the crimes of the Israeli army in the attack on Damascus and Gaza and the assassination of a senior commander of the Quds Brigades, the military wing of the Islamic Jihad, and emphasized the need for a decisive response to this crime.
Palestinian leader Mahmoud Abbas called the Trump administration an obstacle to peace in the Middle East.
Dental tourism is growing rapidly and millions of people travel from countries such as the United Kingdom, the United States and Canada to get affordable dental care.
Most foreign patients come to Korea for cosmetic and cosmetic treatment

Some doctors in China have suggested that it should be subdivided within the scope of cosmetic surgery, such as breast augmentation and other large operations are indeed dangerous in flight, but very small cosmetic surgery such as eyebrows, lips, and double eyelid cuts have no great impact on passengers' own safety.
Nose surgery and Botox injections are at the top of beauty operations
Some organizations and personnel without qualification can carry out related business, illegally practice medicine, or even "black clinics".
The most important benefit of dental tourism is the use of low-cost services, especially if several treatments are required.
Iran ranks 10th in terms of attracting health tourists
A 21-year-old Jiangsu woman spent more than 100,000 yuan to perform breast augmentation, nose augmentation and M lip surgery at a cosmetic hospital in Changzhou, but died on the operating table.
Excessive overtaking of cosmetic surgeries has occurred even during the Coronavirus pandemic.
The hospital where the surgery took place had no rescue equipment and the anesthesiologist had not been registered with the institution, which was an illegal practice.
Dental implants are the best tooth replacement options and the most expensive, which has led to an increase in dental tourism.
More than 2 million foreign patients have come to Korea for medical treatment
A similar case occurred in Ho Chi Minh City, where a 20-year-old woman became blind in her left eye after a rhinoplasty at a beauty salon.
Men are also sometimes candidates for cosmetic surgeries for various reasons
The supply and demand of medical institutions and doctors in China are not matched.
Medical tourism in most cases includes dental services, cosmetic surgery and other types of surgeries
Chinese patients account for 31.2% of all foreign patients who come to Korea for medical treatment
A Chinese female tourist was blinded in her left eye when she underwent plastic filling surgery at a dermatology department in Gangnam-gu, Seoul, South Korea, in 2013.
Correcting complications from unauthorized cosmetic surgeries can be costly and time-consuming.
Restrictions imposed due to coronavirus have been considered in hospitals, but many beauty clinics underground and perhaps in unsanitary conditions, carry out operations.
In dental tourism, people travel abroad to receive dental care, such as a U.S. citizen traveling to Mexico for dental implant surgery or an Australian traveling to Thailand for oral matters.
37.5% of foreign patients go to cosmetic surgery and dermatology hospitals
Two cases of blind girls have occurred due to rhinoplasty failure at "beauty salons" in Vietnam.
Cosmetic surgery in the United Kingdom is very popular, and the number of cosmetic surgeries has risen sharply.
A similar incident occurred at the same plastic surgery institution in July 2020, when Ms. Huang went for breast augmentation surgery and ended up in the intensive care unit of the hospital.
In Hongkou District, a pocket park was built after a number of illegal buildings were demolished.
The project aims to improve the quality of life of residents
The park will include children's play facilities and physical facilities for the elderly.
The vacant land will become a pocket small park in the truth street in culture in the future.
With these funds, parks, playgrounds and an accessible environment for people with limited mobility are created in areas and streets where funds are collected.
City Councilman Yuen Cheng has sought to build a landscaping park that will integrate into the nearby community.
Public uses are among the necessities of the urban community and the valuable advantage is social vitality.

The Green Light Park will have six major facilities, including the Sunshine Lawn, the Green Garden Trail, the flood wetland, the rest pavilion rack, the rest forest belt, and the terrace activity square.
A flowerbed with a bridge will be built in the "pocket square" on Enthusiasts street to make it interesting for children to play.
3 million yuan of funds will be sought to open up the Pocket Park in Tamsui District.
The project will enhance the urban living space with Zhunan and Toufen sports parks as the center

New Taipei Civil Affairs Minister Ke Ching-chung said that in traditional urban areas with high population densities such as Xinbei Banqiao, Xinzhuang, Wugu, and Sanchong, most park facilities have reached the end of use.
The park features a stage for concerts, children's horizontal bars with an original design, hammocks for recreation, sports simulators, parking lots, and modern lighting.
Old trees will be cut down and green spaces will be renewed in the park.

14 parks have been designed and completed, and construction began in August, including Southern Shu Tsai Feet, Nanping, Eastern Leming, Beitun Dunhua, Tanzi, Li Lin, Shipai, Dongshi, Shuimei, Waipu, Dadong, Dajia, Xianda, Ming Dao, Xingxiang, Autonomous.
Some of the areas needed to develop green space in dense tissues of the region in order to increase the per capita green space is on the agenda and will be operational this year.
A "communion park" in the neighboring community is important for children to have fun without worrying about health problems, physical and mental conditions.
A children's playground will be built in the center of the "pocket square" on Enthusiasts street.
Taichung City Xitun District Chaogui Park launches friendly and inclusive park improvement plan.
Community parks should be regarded as part of the urban space network, not an isolated space unit.
The access line in Xindian Qiaoren Park has adopted barrier-free design to provide a more diverse recreational environment.
Another "pocket square" will be arranged in the courtyard of the house on Lyatoshinsky Street, 26-b in the Goloseevsky district of the capital.

Tarti General Manager Ibrahim Ehren said: "We always put our finger on scripts and products that support the country's history, culture, ethics and human values and we will continue to play that role."
Turkey has turned to Somalia to compensate for the influence it lost in Sudan following the departure of former President Omar al-Bashir, who was a strategic ally of Erdogan.
Turkey has a significant presence in Uzbekistan, Tajikistan, and Afghanistan, with Turkish language and literature taught in several universities.
Getty Images, Saudi Arabia ’ s largest private media agency, MBC, has announced it will stop broadcasting Turkish TV series.
Turkey has contributed more than 150 TV series to an estimated 500 million viewers in 146 countries around the world.
According to the Cinema Zone, the cultural group of the Young Journalists Club, the increasing influence of satellite networks among Iranian families has caused significant cultural damage.
Armenia, Greece, UAE, Bahrain, Cyprus, Georgia, Abkhazia, Ossetia, Syria, Yemen, Oman, Iraq, Kuwait, Lebanon, Egypt, Libya are countries listed as part of Turkey's sphere of influence expansion.
The Turkish government has established a strong cultural base in many European countries.
According to Anadolu News Agency, the television series Umm Harun and Montazer 7, which are broadcast on Saudi MBC during Ramadan, has angered citizens.
Turkish television series have reached South America after capturing the film market of the Indian subcontinent, the Middle East, the Caucasus, Central Asia and the Balkans.
TV critic Sina Kluoğlu in the Milliat newspaper added: "Advertising in this type of series is natural. The point is that it should be done cautiously."

The economic, cultural and political weakness and isolation of the Central Asian republics and the Republic of Azerbaijan encouraged Turkish politicians and political parties to seek to attract these countries and pursue their unity within a larger sphere.
Turkey is trying to draw the countries of Turkestan into its game.
In 2018, the Saudi government and MBC stopped buying Turkish drama, describing “Noor ” as “anti-Islamic ” and “subversive ”.
Turkish TV series are the most popular among all series in Uzbekistan, having overtaken both Korean and local Uzbek series.
The Ministry of Culture and Islamic Guidance advances two views together, opportunities and risks, in the field of home serials.
Turkey aims to create a quasi-state that includes Azerbaijan, all the countries of Central Asia, and a number of regions of the Russian Federation.
Turkey established the Agency for Turkic Cooperation and Development to include former Soviet republics of Central Asia in its zone of influence.
The imam-khatib of the cathedral mosque "Mirza Yusuf" of the Yunusabad district of Tashkent, Rahmatullo Saifuddinov, said that Turkish series adversely affect the gene pool of the nation.
The popularity of Turkish series in the Middle East or the Caucasus may be due to cultural and historical commonalities.
Some Arab television shows produced this year do not respect the Palestinian issue and seek to spread ideas that call for co-existence with the occupiers and question the Palestinian cause.
Turkey’s influence in Somalia has been increasing recently.
Turkey sought to influence the cultural and social sphere in Central Asia from the beginning of the collapse of the Soviet Union.
The final episode of the series is an allusion to Eren Belbel, a boy who was said to have been killed in northern Turkey in 2017 by the "illegal" Kurdish Workers' Party (PKK).
The Turkish series dubbed and broadcast by satellite networks provide sexist, pornographic, aristocratic and corrupt narratives of Turkish families.

A test vehicle for extracting oxygen from the Martian atmosphere, as well as a "space helicopter" that is set to fly for the first time on a planet other than Earth, are among those on the Perseverance rover.
The Mars oxygen field resource utilization experimental instrument, MOXIE, makes 5 grams of oxygen from carbon dioxide in the Martian atmosphere.
Musk has a bold idea for solving the Martian atmosphere: detonating a nuclear weapon at the north and south poles of Mars
Oxygen obtained from the Martian atmosphere can be used for breathing and as fuel for rockets.
The Perseverance rover will bring back to Earth samples of Mars rocks.
The robotic helicopter on board the Mars rover successfully completed its first flight on Mars on the 19th of this month, rising 3 meters and suspended for 30 seconds.
Mars Oxygen In-Situ Resource Utilization Experiment (MOXIE) is a device that decomposes carbon dioxide in the Martian atmosphere into carbon monoxide and oxygen.
This is the first time that humans have used instruments in outer space to convert other gases into oxygen.
At present, the amount of carbon dioxide on Mars is not enough to make it habitable for humans.
The Perseverance rover has a device for obtaining oxygen from the Martian atmosphere.

The Ingenuity helicopter will be carried on the belly of the perseverance spacecraft, which will be separated from the spacecraft after landing, and will initially fly five times in a month, each time for about 90 seconds.
The MOXIE system can convert 96% of the carbon dioxide in the Martian atmosphere into oxygen.
Carrying oxygen and fuel far from Earth was not feasible in the past
NASA is developing a rocket to take people to Mars and is experimenting with producing oxygen on the planet.
The Perseverance rover will conduct an experiment to obtain oxygen from the atmosphere of Mars.
MOXIE can obtain up to 10 grams of oxygen per hour
MOXIE is a device as small as a toaster
Traces of methane have already been found on Mars.
Such devices may one day provide astronauts with breathable air
The atmosphere of Mars contains only 0.13% oxygen, compared to 21% on Earth.
“Perseverance” extracted oxygen from the atmosphere of Mars.
Atmospheric pressure and temperature on Mars change along with the oxygen content.
The amount of compensation for each case that has been settled will be in the millions to thousands of dollars.
Bayer announced plans in June to settle all legal lawsuits against Monsanto for $12 billion.
The World Health Organization’s Agency for Research on Cancer rated glyphosate as "possibly carcinogenic" in 2015.
A court in the United States halved compensation for cancer patients from the herbicide Roundup
Since the first Roundup carcinogenicity conviction, Bayer has lost nearly 46% of its market value and its share price has fallen to 2012 levels.
The agreement was made with an estimated 50,000 to 85,000 plaintiffs.

The wave of lawsuits in the United States against Bayer came after the company acquired Monsanto, a producer of seeds and pesticides, in 2018, a $63 billion deal that was the company ’ s largest foreign transaction in Germany.
The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) of the United States claims that glyphosate is safe when used cautiously
A lawsuit in 2019 found that a Roundup user, Edwin Hardeman, received $5.3 million in damages from Monsanto for cancer.

German chemical-pharmaceutical transnational corporation Bayer published a report for the third quarter of 2020 fiscal year, which states that the manufacturer of medicines suffered due to the coronavirus pandemic, resulting in net losses of the company amounted to $3.2 billion, and revenue decreased by 4.2% to $36.8 billion.
The claimants have not yet formally agreed to accept this compensation
Since Bayer acquired Monsanto for US $63 billion, it has faced about 18,400 lawsuits in the United States.
Glyphosate is currently the most popular herbicide in the world, despite concerns over its safety.
German pharmaceutical company Bayer said its U.S. company Monsanto production of herbicide "Roundup" reported cases of cancer in the United States in the second quarter of about 5,000 new cases.
Bayer reported a net loss of €2.7 billion in the third quarter.
Bayer has settled 88,500 of 125,000 claims.
Bayer, the German chemical company, said on Wednesday that a legal dispute over cancer caused by its Roundup brand herbicide has reached a $2 billion settlement with regulators.
A court in the United States ruled that Bayer should pay $289.2 million to school gardener Diwayne Johnson, who contracted cancer as a result of the use of the herbicide Roundup.
Bayer posts loss in second quarter due to large litigation reserve
An agreement to settle a large number of lawsuits was terminated earlier this month.
Germany's Bayer company bought US rival Monsanto for $66 billion in 2018, and Monsanto's product Roundup was also part of the acquisition.
Hundreds of lawsuits against Monsanto for injuries and premature death are moving forward after a scientific analysis was heard in court detailing the carcinogenicity of the Roundup herbicide.
Bayer’s stock price has fallen 42% since the acquisition was completed in June 2018.
The painting found is indeed the “Portrait of a Lady ” by Gustav Klimt, which was stolen from the Ricci Oddi Gallery of Modern Art in 1997.
French authorities have announced that the painting by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt will be returned to his legal heir because it was sold under Nazi Germany’s oppression.
The picture “Portrait of a Woman ” by the Austrian artist Gustav Klimt lay in the wall for 22 years.

Thieves of a stolen painting by Gustav Klimt, which was recently discovered in the same Italian gallery where it was stolen after 22 years, have admitted to returning the artwork to the scene of the theft.
The painting is estimated at 60 million euros.
The collector bequeathed his collection of art to the city, and after his death, they were exhibited in the gallery, which received his name.

In the Italian city of Piacenza, a gardener peeled ivy off the wall in the Ricci Oddi gallery and accidentally found a painting by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt “Portrait of a Woman ” stolen 22 years ago from the same gallery.
The stolen painting “Portrait of a Lady” by Gustav Klimt was found wrapped in a black cloth bag and hidden by a metal plate in the garden wall of the Ricci Oddia Piacenza Modern Art Museum.

Italian prosecutor Ornella Chicca said the actual painting was verified as authentic, but it remains unclear whether the painting was later smuggled back into the wall mezzanine or was hidden there from the beginning, pending further examination and clarification.

Due to the closure of galleries and museums in Italy due to the spread of the coronavirus, it has not been possible for the public to visit the work in person, and officials of the Ricci Odi Gallery have provided the opportunity to visit the painting in its former exhibition.
Austrian painter Gustav Klimt ’ s Portrait of a Lady was stolen for 23 years.
The painting was stolen on February 22, 1997 from the Ricci Oddi Gallery of Contemporary Art as preparations for the Klimt exhibition were underway.
Guido Cauzzi, an art expert, said that the painting was in good condition and whether it had been hidden inside the wall remained to be investigated.
The painting features a special symbolic pattern.
The stolen painting “Portrait of a Woman ” is Klimt ’ s only painting with a value of 60 million euros.

Two men admitted that 22 years ago they stole and then returned the painting by Gustav Klimt “Portrait of a Woman” and not all the time kept it in a niche in the wall of the Ricci Oddi gallery in Piacenza, Italy, where it was found by gardeners.

French Culture Minister Roselyne Bachelot said that the return of the painting was a recognition of the suffering endured by people at the time, and the work itself was a testimony to the many shattered lives of the Nazi era.
France ’ s Reuters is set to return the painting by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt to the Jewish family of its heir, which the Nazis forced to sell in 1938.
Laurence de Carr returned one of Gustav Klimt's paintings to the original owners, a Jewish family, in 2021.
The Nazis captured Austria in 1938 and stole the family's paintings, including the portrait of Adele Bloch-Bauer.
Drought has affected more than 110,000 hectares of agricultural land in the province

Golestan Agriculture Organization's head of crisis management said that the reduction of rainfall and drought in the current agricultural year caused 277,000 hectares of agricultural land in the province between 10 and 100 percent damage.
The level of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere has reached a critical point
The increase in temperature has caused behavioral changes in the field of agriculture.
The cultivation of low-water crops is being developed in the agricultural lands of Qazvin
The rainfall of Isfahan province decreased by 38% in the current agricultural year
Drought threatens crops of winter and spring cereals in most of the fields in the Republic of Crimea
The level of carbon dioxide in the Earth's atmosphere has reached a historic maximum
Climate change has caused water temperatures in some areas to become hotter more frequently and last longer.
Modification of cultivation pattern is the first priority of Sistan and Baluchestan agricultural sector

The effects of low rainfall in the western part of the country are 15 to 40 percent, according to the rainfall statistics in Kermanshah 42 percent, Kurdistan 19 percent, East Azerbaijan 14 percent, West Azerbaijan 13 percent, Ardebil 8 percent, Alborz 10 percent and Tehran 15 percent less than normal.
Drought has resulted in the loss of 21,500 hectares of crops cultivated in the agricultural lands of the cereals
Human activity influences precipitation intensity.
Dangerous heat waves will occur more often due to global warming.
Farmers should change their management of cultivation.
A 46 percent decrease in rainfall from the beginning of the agricultural year to today, compared to long-term statistics, caused 82 percent of the area of northern Khorasan to be affected by drought
This year's flood has damaged more than 100,000 hectares of agricultural and horticultural land in the province
Climate change is putting food security at risk.
The temperature has increased by more than 2.5 degrees Celsius.
Plans have been made to reduce the level of cultivation of high-water crops every year and replace them with other crops that have economic justification for farmers.
Rainfall in West Azerbaijan has halved compared to last year’s crop
This year's drought is likely to have an adverse impact on crop growth.
Water can help fight climate change.
Climate change will increase the severity of extreme climatic events.
Reforming the cultivation pattern of crops by replacing and developing drought resistant plants is another of the main programs of the Agricultural Jihad Organization of Isfahan
The Golden Bear for Best Film of the 70th Berlinale goes to "Sheytan vojud nadarad" (There Is No Evil), directed by Mohammad Rasoulof.
The film explores our responsibilities and choices in life.

The Berlin Film Festival officials have decided to present an image of Iranian society as well as the political and cultural struggles of Iranians to achieve individual and social freedoms by presenting a film collection from the past 50 years of Iranian cinema.
Iranian film Innocence wins Golden Bear at Berlin Film Festival
The Iranian film “There is no Evil ” won the Golden Bear Award at the Berlin Film Festival, and the director Mohammad Rasoulof was also unable to leave the country.
Jafar Panahi's film "Offside" won several awards, including the Silver Bear of the Berlin Festival.
This film narrates the true story of a person named Hossein Sabzian who introduces himself as a well-known director.
The festival is regarded as the most political among the prestigious film festivals around the world
Mina, a thirty-five-year-old woman whose husband was killed for murder, is now living a difficult and miserable life

Iranian filmmakers such as Sohrab Shahid-Sales and Asghar Farhadi received Silver Bear and Golden Bear at the Berlin Film Festival for Dead Nature and Nader and Simin ’ s Divorce in 1974 and 2011, respectively.
Innocence won the Golden Bear Award, the highest honor at the Berlin Film Festival, for the topic of the death penalty in Iran.
The film "The Devil Does Not Exist" was made in secret.
The film was present in the Berlin competition section and was the only representative of Iranian cinema at this year ’ s Berlin Film Festival.
A man comes to the police and makes a confession that puts Mina ’ s life in a new direction
Iranian cinema had won the Berlin Golden Bear with the film “A Separation from Simin ” by Asghar Farhadi.
The film won the Golden Bear at the Berlin International Film Festival in 2017 and brought the director to fame.
The film won an Amnesty International award.
The Iranian film “Final Zone ” has entered the competition section of the Berlin Film Festival
The film leads to a terrible catastrophe in the end.

Several of Mohammad Rasoul Oof's previous films, including "Hope to See You" and "Manuscripts Don't Burn," have never been shown in Iran, despite appearances at some international festivals, including the Cannes Film Festival.
The film "A Separation" won the Golden Bear and two Silver Bears in 2011.
The film The Evil and Their Distance breaks the boundaries of national and cultural restrictions.

The festival’s judges, including Iranian director Mohammad Rasoul Oof, Italian documentary filmmaker John Franco Roseniya, director Yasmila Zabenić, Hungarian filmmaker Ildiko Enid, Romanian director Adina Pintiel and Ndaf Lapid, watched the films from their homes.
The film was shot outside of Iran.
Asghar Farhadi presented Iran at the Oscars Film Festival with the film The Salesman in 2017 and won the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.

At least four people were killed, including two children aged five and eight, and 15 others were rescued when a boat carrying human snakes sank off the British Channel off Dunkirk, France, on Tuesday.
The UK government has announced a £54 million agreement with the French government to coordinate between the two countries to prevent migrants from crossing the French border and entering the UK.
235 illegal migrants in 17 boats attempting to cross the English Channel from northern France were intercepted by British Coast Guard and Border Guard officers on Thursday.
The boats carried immigrants from Iran, Iraq, and Afghanistan.

A British Home Office official revealed that authorities are considering a series of measures to stop illegal immigrants from sneaking into the UK by boat through the British Channel to apply for asylum
17 Iranian migrants made their way to British shores early Tuesday after hijacking a French fishing boat and crossing the English Channel, but were stopped by coast guard forces.
The French government will double the number of police patrols on its shores as part of an agreement with Britain to stop migrants from crossing the Channel
British border guards and the coast guard rescued about 50 refugees who were trying to get into the country from France, sailing across the English Channel in rubber boats.
The Calais refugee camp is home to illegal migrants who cross the sea from Africa and the Middle East to Europe, concentrated in Calais, waiting for a chance to travel to the UK.
The UK government has developed a new immigration policy plan that states that people who enter the UK illegally to seek asylum will not have the same rights as those who enter legally.
An Iranian refugee waiting in France to enter Britain reported that four members of an Iranian family of five died in the English Channel, and the body of their 15-month-old baby has not been found.
Home Office officials will meet with French officials this week to pressure the French side to stop illegal immigration in the British Channel.
Nearly 100 refugees crossed the English Channel this week by boat, trying to reach Britain from France to seek asylum.
Most of the illegal refugees in Calais are from Syria, Eritrea, Sudan and Ethiopia.
British Prime Minister Boris Johnson warned refugees not to cross the Channel on Friday, saying "we will send you back" and that the law would treat them as "illegal immigrants".

Five members of a family called Irannejad, including 9-year-old Anita Irannejad, 6-year-old Armin Irannejad and 15-month-old Artin Irannejad, died in the recent sinking of refugee boats in the English Channel.
Britain’s immigration minister said the government was working faster with France to toughen immigration measures to stop migrants crossing the Channel illegally.
British services intercepted more than 80 migrants trying to reach the shores of the United Kingdom by boat through the English Channel.
A group of refugees trying to cross the English Channel were rescued by French border guards

The Minister of immigration control of Great Britain Chris Philp spoke about the intention of the government to stop the flow of refugees and return to France those who have already managed to cross the English Channel.
The British Border Protection Agency found two boats carrying nine and 11 people, claiming to be from countries such as Iran, Guinea and Kuwait.

France's Interior Minister Christophe Castaner and his British counterpart Sajid Javid met on Sunday to discuss the expansion of cooperation between the two countries to deal with the illegal passage of asylum seekers across the Channel Sea.
The issue of migrants attempting to reach the UK via the English Channel on kayaks, inflatable boats and small vessels is nothing new, and dozens have been rescued and detained in recent months.
Migrants tried to make their way through the tunnel to the UK from the French port of Calais

Home Secretary Sajid David cut short his Christmas holiday with his family in Africa on Sunday and returned home to tackle a difficult situation with an unexpected wave of illegal migrants arriving in Britain by boat from France.
Stratolaunch was designed to launch space rockets.
The wingspan of the Stratolaunch aircraft reaches 117 m
The world’s largest aircraft made its first flight
The central part of the Stratolaunch aircraft is designed to mount space rockets
The world's largest aircraft has six engines
The world ’ s largest aircraft took off for the second time.
The Stratolaunch aircraft made the first test flight on Saturday, April 13
The Stratolaunch aircraft has two fuselages and a wingspan of 117 meters, which is more than the wingspan of any other aircraft that has ever taken to the sky.
The demand for launching satellites is increasing.
Stratolaunch is planned to be used as an aerial platform for launching launch vehicles to deliver cargo to low Earth orbit.
The world ’ s largest aircraft has a wingspan longer than a football field.
The test of the Stratolaunch aircraft passed successfully
The Stratolaunch aircraft is designed to launch launch vehicles from an altitude of 9100 m.
The Stratolaunch aircraft has a mass of 226.8 tons.
It’s about sending the aircraft to the edge of space and beyond
Stratolaunch was tested on the runway.
The test flight was recognized by experts as successful.
The prototype of the Stratolaunch aircraft made several runs on the runway during testing.
Air-launched rocket technology has great military value
Stratolaunch plans to carry future spacecraft to high altitude.
The company overcame a new milestone.
A woman with a poster tried to get into the frame in front of the peloton and was hit by a German cyclist Tony Martin.
A fan with a poster provoked a mass accident at the Tour de France
Nine drivers, including German driver Jasha Suetterlin, were injured in the accident, and Jasha Suetterlin withdrew from the race and was unable to compete in the second race.

French military and police authorities announced that they would conduct an investigation into the incident, which resulted in involuntary injury due to a deliberate breach of safety obligations, and that it would be completed within three months.
The accident happened 45 kilometers from the finish when a woman wearing a bright yellow coat with sunglasses stood by the track holding a card that read “ALLEZ OPI-OMI ” in French.
The spectator, a woman wearing a bright yellow coat, caused two championship contenders, Sonny Colbrelli and Wout van Aert, to get involved in the accident and lose time.
The incident took place in the first leg of the Tour de France, between the cities of Britanny and Landerneau in the north-west.
Another accident occurred with less than 10 kilometres left in the race, forcing Chris Froome and several drivers to retire.
The woman has been charged with unintentional harm and putting the lives of others at risk.
The 30-year-old French woman who caused the accident has been arrested and will face charges from the organizers.
The fan did not notice the approaching cyclists when trying to show a cardboard sign with an inscription in the camera.
French police arrested the culprit of the mass fall of the participants of the Tour de France
Another accident occurred 8 kilometers from the end, injuring up to 12 people, including famous driver Chris Froome, who will withdraw from the competition.
If the affected riders file lawsuits against her, the punishment may be more severe - up to a year in prison.
The woman immediately left the scene after the accident, but surrendered to the police a few days later and has been detained.
The accident was caused by a fan who went out on the track with a poster to attract the attention of television viewers.
The first stage of the Tour de France started in Brest
A case for “unintentional infliction of severe bodily injuries caused by a gross violation of safety rules ” was initiated against the woman.

Iran is prepared to pay compensation to the families of the victims of Ukraine International Airlines Flight PS752, which was shot down near Tehran in January.
The court of Canada recognized the downing of the UIA aircraft in Iran as a terrorist act

The plaintiffs, who lost family members in the incident, sued Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, as well as Iran's Revolutionary Guards and armed forces, demanding $1.5 billion Canadian dollars in compensation.

Amir Ali Hajizadeh, commander of the Revolutionary Guards Aerospace Force, claimed responsibility for the fatal error, saying that the IRGC air defense officer had “mistakenly identified the Ukrainian passenger plane as a cruise missile” and fired at it.

Ukraine’s foreign minister said that if there is no agreement with Iran about the Ukrainian plane incident, it is possible to launch a legal process against Iran along with the countries involved in the incident

A spokesman for Iran’s foreign ministry said the Canadian court had no jurisdiction to investigate the Ukrainian plane crash or a possible failure in an incident that occurred outside Canada’s territory.
Some relatives of the dead passengers of the UIA flight PS752, shot down by the Iranian military, filed a lawsuit against Iran in the Federal Court in Washington.
Iran shot down the Ukrainian plane with flight number PS-752 on January 8, 2019, using at least two IRGC defense missiles, resulting in the death of all 176 people on board.
Coordination is currently underway for negotiations on the amount of compensation by the Islamic Republic of Iran to the victims of the incident, the Ukrainian airline and the aircraft manufacturers.
A court in Ontario, Canada, ruled that Iran's actions were deliberate "terrorism" based on written evidence provided by the family.

A Washington-based legal office filed a complaint on behalf of relatives of the victims of Ukraine International Airlines Flight 752 who were American citizens against the Islamic Republic government led by Ali Khamenei and the Revolutionary Guards under the command of Hossein Salami.
Ukraine International Airlines Flight 752 was hit by two Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps missiles in January 2020.
The ambassador of the Islamic Republic of Iran to Ukraine presented the report of the third round of negotiations between Iran and Ukraine on the incident of the Ukrainian plane crash.

The Court of Appeals dismissed the arguments of Iranian government lawyers, accusing the Islamic Republic of seeking to undermine the Justice for Victims of Terrorism Act passed by the Canadian Parliament.
The National Bank of Iran filed an appeal against a lower court ruling in the United States that allowed victims of terrorism to claim millions of dollars in compensation from the bank.
Hassan Rezaeifar, the head of the Civil Aviation Organization ’ s Accidents Commission, said the payment of damages depends on the Iran-Ukraine agreement.

Whaling is part of the Japanese tradition.
Japan made the decision to withdraw from the International Whaling Commission last year
Japan begins third year of commercial whaling

Although the dolphin season in Taiji has been criticized by the outside world, the Japanese government has authorized the killing or capture of more than 16,000 whales and dolphins, including dolphins, baleen whales and short-leg pilot whales, and this year has resumed commercial whaling.
Japan continued whale hunting under the guise of scientific activity
Japan has a tradition of eating whale meat and has become the main target of environmental groups every year.
These whales are hunted annually in the Southern Ocean.
Japan will stop survey fishing in the Antarctic Sea
Japan has resumed whaling to sell its meat this year despite international protests.

The decision of the Japanese authorities was criticized by activists and countries not engaged in whaling
Japan recently resumed commercial whaling in defiance of international criticism.

China, the United States, Russia, India, Japan, South Korea and the European Union signed the ITER plan to build a superconducting Kamake-type fusion reactor in Cadarache, southern France, to explore the scientific and engineering feasibility of using fusion energy for peaceful purposes.
China is accelerating its own pace of development, with the goal of designing a fully fledged fusion reactor capable of generating electricity.
